Klasse CPUPlayer

java.lang.Object
players.PlayerEntity
players.CPUPlayer
public class CPUPlayer extends PlayerEntity
Represents a CPU player in the game, which operates based on a specific strategy. The CPU player uses a provided difficulty strategy to calculate its move and removal operations. It simulates actions as if it were a human player, with a slight delay to mimic real-time decisions.

  • Felddetails

    • strategy

      private DifficultyStrategy strategy

    • opponent

      private PlayerEntity opponent

  • Konstruktordetails

    • CPUPlayer

      public CPUPlayer(String name, PlayerEntity opponent)
      Constructs a CPU player with the specified name and an opposing player. The CPU player uses its strategy to calculate moves and removals during gameplay.
      Parameter:
      name - The name of the CPU player.
      opponent - The opponent player that the CPU player will compete against.

  • Methodendetails

    • makeMove

      public Node[] makeMove()
      Calculates the next move for the current CPU player based on the defined strategy. This method creates imaginative representations of the CPU player and its opponent to simulate possible moves and evaluate them using the assigned strategy.

      To mimic the behavior of a human player, the method includes a delay before determining and returning the calculated move.

      If the sleeping thread is interrupted, an informational message is logged, and the method returns null.

      Gibt zurück:
      An array of Node objects representing the calculated move, or null if the process is interrupted.

    • makeRemove

      public Node makeRemove()
      Calculates the next removal action for the current CPU player based on the defined strategy. This method creates imaginative representations of the CPU player and its opponent to simulate possible removal scenarios and evaluate them using the assigned strategy.

      To mimic the behavior of a human player, the method includes a delay before determining and returning the calculated removal.

      If the sleeping thread is interrupted, an informational message is logged, and the method returns null.

      Gibt zurück:
      A Node object representing the calculated removal action, or null if the process is interrupted.

    • setDifficulty

      public void setDifficulty(DifficultyStrategy strategy)
      Sets the difficulty level of the CPU player by assigning a new strategy. The strategy determines how the CPU player calculates moves and removals during gameplay.
      Parameter:
      strategy - The difficulty strategy to be applied to the CPU player's behavior.

    • getDifficulty

      public DifficultyStrategy getDifficulty()
      Retrieves the difficulty strategy currently assigned to the CPU player. The difficulty strategy determines how the CPU player makes decisions during gameplay.
      Gibt zurück:
      The DifficultyStrategy assigned to the CPU player.
